title: Driver driver conflict on false-memory array
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

The enclosed ZIP [mcve-array.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/2917378/mcve-array.zip) file contains a short verilog file and an sby file.  Run SymbiYosys on the sby file.  

## Expected behavior

The mcve.v verilog file contains a reference to a 2D array written as a memory, with per clock access to each element of the array.  the code is similar to the code shown below.

    reg [LGCOUNT-1:0] counters [0:NS-1];

    generate for(N=0; N<NS; N=N+1)
    begin

        always @(posedge i_clk)
        if (i_stb[N])
            counters[N] <= counters[N]+1;

    end endgenerate

Obviously, `counters[N]` cannot be implemented as a memory.  It should instead be transformed into an LGCOUNT x NS array of FFs.  If yosys cannot do so, it should create an error and fail to implement the design as written.



## Actual behavior

Yosys tries to create a memory for accessing counters, and then produces tons of driver/driver conflicts.
